events.title: World at ‘perilous moment’ as leaders warn HIV gains are at risk
events.description: Four decades after AIDS first emerged as a global crisis, world leaders, advocates and community representatives gathered at UN Headquarters on Monday issued a stark warning: progress against HIV is slowing just as financial pressures and shrinking support threaten to reverse decades of gains.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World at ‘perilous moment’ as leaders warn HIV gains are at risk

events.title: Global learning losses threaten an entire generation
events.description: Conflict, displacement and climate shocks are disrupting education for an estimated 258 million school-aged children and adolescents worldwide, raising fears that millions risk losing not only years of schooling but future opportunities altogether, according to a new report released Tuesday.

events.title: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
events.description: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.
events.ticker_label: 💥 At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion

events.title: Offshore typhoon threatens Philippine provinces with possible flooding and rough seas
events.description: Philippine authorities have warned of possible flooding, landslides and rough seas in the country’s two northernmost provinces as a powerful typhoon roared offshore
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Offshore typhoon threatens Philippine provinces with possible flooding and rough seas

events.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
events.description: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the world. The officials said the outbreak, now the third largest on record, required “strong immediate support”, but that the global risk remained low. Continue reading...

events.title: Niger becomes the third country to leave the International Criminal Court
events.description: Niger has formally left the International Criminal Court after accusing the global tribunal of what it says is selective justice

events.title: Counterterrorism officials investigating after suspect goes on rampage in alleged hate attack: report
events.description: Counterterrorism officials are investigating after a suspect allegedly went on an anti-Muslim rampage in Edinburgh, leaving five men injured near a mosque and another location.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Counterterrorism officials investigating after suspect goes on rampage in alleged hate attack: report

events.title: Canadian police warn of possible copycat attacks after deadly shootout in Montreal
events.description: Assailant behind shooting that left three people dead wrote ‘incel’-like manifesto that was posted by a far-right outlet Police in Canada are warning of possible copycat attacks after three people died in a shootout in Montreal and the assailant’s lengthy manifesto, which called for “a new bloodletting”, was posted online by a far-right outlet. The document contains many of the hallmark grievances of the “involuntary celibacy” – or “incel” – movement in addition racist and misogynistic conspiracy theories. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canadian police warn of possible copycat attacks after deadly shootout in Montreal

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Warnings of ‘dangerous cycle of escalation’ in Ukraine
events.description: Deadly drone and missile attacks in Ukraine over the weekend represented a “dangerous cycle of escalation” in Russia’s full-scale four-year-old war, the Security Council heard on Monday from Mohamed Khaled Khiari, Assistant Secretary-General in the Departments of Political and Peacebuilding Affairs and Peace Operations, while Edem Wosornu, crisis response director at the UN relief office, OCHA, warned that “the choices made here can mean the difference between lives saved or lives lost.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Warnings of ‘dangerous cycle of escalation’ in Ukraine

events.title: Myanmar: Aid decline compounds suffering amid ongoing military attacks
events.description: A drop in humanitarian assistance is worsening the suffering of millions of people in Myanmar after five years of conflict-related violence, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report published on Monday.

events.title: 6 dead, 39 shot in Chicago bloodbath as Trump touts DC-style cleanup where National Guard stepped in
events.description: President Trump offers to make Chicago safe in one month after a violent weekend left six dead and 39 injured in dozens of shootings citywide.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 6 dead, 39 shot in Chicago bloodbath as Trump touts DC-style cleanup where National Guard stepped in

events.title: Tasmanian devils, swift parrots, black swans: the animals at risk if bird flu takes off in Australia
events.description: Federal government identifies more than 150 native and unique bird species and 10 mammal species at ‘very high risk’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast More than 150 of Australia’s native and unique bird species have been assessed as being at “very high risk” of extinction or major decline if they catch the deadly H5N1 bird flu strain, with Western Australia’s celebrated black swans among the most susceptible. The federal government analysis of Australia’s 800 different birds and 350 mammals reflects the high level of concern among experts about the arrival of the H5N1 strain, which has killed millions of birds and mammals around the world. Continue reading...

events.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
events.description: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades race In the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza , first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire. Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

events.title: India: What’s driving the intense pressure on students?
events.description: The reported deaths of aspiring medical students in India have exposed the immense pressures of the country's high-stakes examination system and a worsening mental-health crisis.

events.title: Qatar gas facility explosion kills 13 and leaves dozens injured
events.description: At least 13 people are dead and dozens injured after an explosion at a key Qatar gas facility previously targeted by Iranian strikes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Qatar gas facility explosion kills 13 and leaves dozens injured

events.title: World News in Brief: Pope Leo urges action on hunger, humanitarian strain deepens in Gaza, families return to Lebanon
events.description: Pope Leo called on the international community to renew its commitment to tackling hunger and malnutrition, describing access to adequate food as a “fundamental human right” during a visit to the World Food Programme (WFP) headquarters in Rome on Monday.

events.title: LISTEN: Mom’s 911 call helps feds tap out suspect in White House UFC terror plot
events.description: Newly released 911 calls reveal an Ohio mother's warnings about her son's suspected extremist beliefs months before the alleged UFC White House plot.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 LISTEN: Mom’s 911 call helps feds tap out suspect in White House UFC terror plot

events.title: Boy, 12, killed after suspect opened fire during brawl near theater district, police say
events.description: A 12-year-old boy, Damarion Morehead, was killed in a Nashville theater district shooting after a brawl broke out, police say. Suspect Devin Orr arrested.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Boy, 12, killed after suspect opened fire during brawl near theater district, police say

events.title: Tesla crash that killed a woman under US federal investigation
events.description: A Tesla driver on Friday said he was using ‘self-driving’ technology after his vehicle sped into a Texas home.

events.title: As heatwave sweeps across Europe, UN urges AI firms to 'come clean' on environmental footprint
events.description: UN chief Antonio Guterres called on June 23 for faster action on global warming, challenging AI firms to "come clean" about their environmental footprint and warning that fossil fuels were driving climate and energy crises. This comes as Europe bakes under a second heatwave in as many months. A UN study earlier in June found that the facilities consumed more electricity than all but 10 countries in 2025. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 As heatwave sweeps across Europe, UN urges AI firms to 'come clean' on environmental footprint

events.title: French heatwave proves deadly as country struggles to adapt
events.description: As France experienced its hottest day yet in its latest heatwave, two children, aged 2 and 4 were found dead inside a parked car in the country's south. It comes after three elderly people died in their homes from heat, according to authorities. Temperatures were forecast to reach up to 43 degrees Celsius across the territory on June 22 and some 39 million people are now under "red alert" warnings for extreme heat.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 French heatwave proves deadly as country struggles to adapt

events.title: Tunisian inflation grows, driven up by a rise in food prices
events.description: In Tunisia, inflation topped 5 percent last month, driven by a significant rise in food prices. One cause is supply chain issues and middlemen hiking prices. The crisis is exacerbated by continuing high unemployment rates, especially in marginalized regions far from the capital and the coast. Protests these days often feature slogans about the high cost of living. FRANCE 24's Lilia Blaise and Hamdi Tlili report.

events.title: Global Ebola cases top 1,000 as UN races to reach DR Congo’s most vulnerable
events.description: As global confirmed Ebola cases reach 1,000, nearly three million children and adolescents are at risk in the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while efforts increase to treat prisoners near the epicentre of the current outbreak, UN agencies warned on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Global Ebola cases top 1,000 as UN races to reach DR Congo’s most vulnerable

events.title: ‘Better future for Syria remains within reach,’ UN Security Council hears
events.description: Syria has emerged after more than a decade of crisis with “clear signs of progress”, but acute needs remain and efforts must boost support so the country can “move from survival towards recovery”, UN officials told the Security Council on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Better future for Syria remains within reach,’ UN Security Council hears

events.title: Australia ‘sleepwalking’ into AI crisis and ‘tech bro free-for-all’, says Greens senator
events.description: Sarah Hanson Young’s warning comes as David Pocock urges government to prevent firms using Australian content to train AI models Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The independent senator David Pocock has challenged the Albanese government to prevent tech giants using Australian content to train AI models as cabinet considers proposals to change copyright rules for the rapidly developing technology. His call came as the Greens senator Sarah Hanson-Young called for a moratorium on the building and approval of new datacentres in Australia until “we get the regulations right”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia ‘sleepwalking’ into AI crisis and ‘tech bro free-for-all’, says Greens senator

events.title: Ethiopia's ruling party secures 90% of votes in national election
events.description: Ethiopian Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's Prosperity Party won a landslide victory in legislative polls, giving it almost 90 percent of the seats. Abiy has led Ethiopia since 2018, and is increasingly criticised for growing authoritarianism. Northern Tigray, still recovering from a devastating 2020-22 conflict that claimed 600,000 lives, did not vote. Growing tensions between local and federal authorities have raised fears of a renewed conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Ethiopia's ruling party secures 90% of votes in national election

events.title: Scores of drones target Moscow as Russian strikes on Ukraine kill six
events.description: Dozens of drones were shot down as they headed for Moscow overnight, while six people were killed in Russian attacks on Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Scores of drones target Moscow as Russian strikes on Ukraine kill six

events.title: Suspect 'neutralized' after Montreal shooting leaves at least 2 dead including officer
events.description: Authorities in Montreal neutralized a suspected gunman after a fatal shooting Monday in the Côte-des-Neiges neighborhood left two people dead.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Suspect 'neutralized' after Montreal shooting leaves at least 2 dead including officer

events.title: A U.S.-Iran dispute over nuclear inspections clouds work to finalize a war-ending deal
events.description: As U.S.-Iran talks continued, a break in the shipping bottleneck through the Strait of Hormuz appeared to be in the works.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A U.S.-Iran dispute over nuclear inspections clouds work to finalize a war-ending deal

events.title: Europe heat wave: France measures hottest-ever day
events.description: Monuments in Paris are working on reduced hours, and dozens of people in France have drowned during the heat wave. Britain, France, Italy and Spain have issued red alerts and health warnings. More on DW.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Europe heat wave: France measures hottest-ever day

events.title: Climate crisis: UN chief lays out solutions blueprint for clean energy transition
events.description: As a deadly heatwave continued to grip Europe on Tuesday, UN Secretary-General António Guterres issued an impassioned appeal for more ambitious global action on climate change caused by fossil fuels, to prevent irreversible damage.

events.title: Russia preparing hybrid attacks on NATO's eastern flank, intelligence warns
events.description: Latvian intelligence warns Russia is preparing military provocations against Baltic states or Poland, including drones and missiles, to pressure NATO.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia preparing hybrid attacks on NATO's eastern flank, intelligence warns

events.title: Myanmar army killed over 700 civilians in six months, UN says
events.description: The new UN report says the 702 civilian deaths over six months last year included 153 children.

events.title: Who’s being left out of the World Cup?
events.description: Can football’s biggest stage stay global as visa denials and border restrictions mount?

events.title: Ghana conference calls for formal apology for transatlantic slave trade
events.description: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at event includes demand for compensation and debt relief More than money: the logic of slavery reparations A global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade. Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

events.title: Forty people drown in France in a week as country endures hottest night for 79 years
events.description: No description available.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Forty people drown in France in a week as country endures hottest night for 79 years

events.title: From malaria to energy: Why solutions from the Global South aren’t reaching the people who need them most
events.description: Innovators – especially in the Global South – are too often locked out of funding and opportunity despite offering solutions to some of the world’s most pressing challenges.

events.title: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
events.description: It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead

events.title: Lebanese struggle to get by in an ancient city under Israeli evacuation order
events.description: In one of the oldest cities in Lebanon, once besieged by Alexander the Great, ordinary people struggle to survive Israeli attacks.

events.title: Irish company 'assisting in commission of crimes' by supplying alumina to Russia for Ukraine war
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Wayne Jordash, President of Global Rights Compliance Foundation. He argues that the European Union's approach to alumina exports exposes a fundamental flaw: while seeking to constrain Russia's military-industrial capabilities through sanctions, European authorities continue to permit the export of a strategic raw material that may ultimately support Russian weapons production.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Irish company 'assisting in commission of crimes' by supplying alumina to Russia for Ukraine war

events.title: Messi smashes World Cup goal record, Mbappé close behind
events.description: Forwards are delivering at the FIFA World Cup. Argentina's Lionel Messi has become the top scorer in the history of the competition, closely followed by France's Kylian Mbappé. Also highly noticed, Norwegian Erling Haaland, who scored a brace against Senegal. Besides Messi, Mbappé and Haaland, football enthusiasts have their eyes on England's Harry Kane, who scored two goals in his opening match, and was the top scorer at the 2018 tournament.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Messi smashes World Cup goal record, Mbappé close behind

events.title: France inducts Resistance fighter, soldier, historian Marc Bloch into its Panthéon of greats
events.description: Eighty-two years after he was executed by Nazis, Resistance fighter and historian Marc Bloch will be inducted into the Panthéon on Tuesday to repose alongside such luminaries as Voltaire and Jean-Jacques Rousseau. A professor in medieval history, Bloch fought for France in World War I and joined the Resistance in World War II.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 France inducts Resistance fighter, soldier, historian Marc Bloch into its Panthéon of greats

events.title: Sudan: Drone attacks endanger civilians and shut down critical services in El Obeid
events.description: Escalating violence in and around the Sudanese city of El Obeid is putting civilians at increased risk and disrupting essential services, the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Monday.

events.title: Colombia: The 'Tiger's' victory signals a rightward shift
events.description: With the election of Abelardo de la Espriella, an ultra-right-wing outsider has won the presidential runoff in Colombia for the first time. The tight victory marks a political shift and could trigger new conflicts.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Colombia: The 'Tiger's' victory signals a rightward shift

events.title: Eiffel Tower closes early due to heatwave
events.description: The French capital's Eiffel Tower, one of the most visited tourist attractions in the world, has closed early on June 23 because of exceptionally high temperatures. This comes as Paris struggles to cope with a historic heatwave, and residents try to adapt in a city that is not made to withstand such extreme weather. FRANCE 24's Caroline Baum is at the Eiffel Tower and spoke with some of its last visitors for the day.

events.title: Police officer, passer-by and gunman killed in hotel shoot-out
events.description: A police officer has been killed in a shooting at a hotel in Canada that also left a civilian and a suspected gunman dead.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Police officer, passer-by and gunman killed in hotel shoot-out

events.title: Conflicts command headlines…protecting peace, less so: The UN Peacebuilding Fund explained
events.description: Wars regularly make headlines. Preventing them rarely does.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Conflicts command headlines…protecting peace, less so: The UN Peacebuilding Fund explained

events.title: Sudan: Security Council warns of mass atrocity risk in El Obeid
events.description: The UN Security Council has expressed alarm over reports of substantial military reinforcements by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) around El Obeid in Sudan, warning of the risk of a potential ground offensive on the city.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan: Security Council warns of mass atrocity risk in El Obeid

events.title: ICE arrests illegal immigrant Illinois teacher linked to Tren de Aragua mass shooting
events.description: Former Illinois teacher living in the U.S. illegally allegedly drove gunmen to a 2024 Tren de Aragua mass shooting in Chicago that killed three people.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 ICE arrests illegal immigrant Illinois teacher linked to Tren de Aragua mass shooting

events.title: Albanian protesters flood streets demanding prime minister's resignation amid corruption accusations
events.description: Albania protests target Socialist PM Edi Rama over corruption allegations, not Kushner investments, former ambassador tells Fox News Digital.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Albanian protesters flood streets demanding prime minister's resignation amid corruption accusations

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia
events.description: Strong winds and heavy rain batter Slovenia, while France experiences atypical heatwave Severe thunderstorms swept across the Balkans last week, bringing widespread destruction to parts of the region. The storms developed as unstable hot air lingered over the Adriatic Sea while a cold front plunged south-eastward. The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with 23mm reported in Kranj. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia

events.title: Sky glows red as storms sweep Russia
events.description: Eyewitness video showed the skies turning a dramatic shade of red over Miass, a city in Russia’s Chelyabinsk, region on Sunday. It comes as local authorities warned of heavy rain and thunderstorms, following downpours that have swept several Russian regions over the past week.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Sky glows red as storms sweep Russia

events.title: In South Korea, AI chip workers become top marriage prospects
events.description: In South Korea, the AI frenzy has sent the value of the country's top chipmakers skyrocketing. Korea's Samsung Electronics and its chief rival SK Hynix have been trading places as the world's most valuable producer of memory chips. Following some strident union organizing at the firms, their workers have become some of the country's highest-paid - with some performance bonuses at Samsung's chip unit set to top 400,000 dollars this year. That has not only made jobs at the companies highly sought-after: it's also made their employees highly sought-after as marriage prospects.

events.title: A red alert over France, and heat that may rewrite the record books
events.description: Millions in France are enduring extreme heat, with temperatures soaring and 40 drowning deaths reported since June 18

events.title: Air traffic control audio captures tense moment two planes nearly collided at Boston Logan Airport
events.description: On Saturday, a Delta Air Lines plane and an American Airline plane came within 300 feet of colliding on the runway of Boston Logan International Airport.

events.title: Inquiry ordered after building fire kills 15 in north India city
events.description: Witnesses described people jumping from windows to escape, while rescue teams broke through a wall to save lives.

events.title: Russian troop build-up threatens city seen as key to seizing Ukraine's Donbas
events.description: If Kostyantynivka falls, Russian forces would be able push towards Ukraine's last remaining strongholds in the east.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russian troop build-up threatens city seen as key to seizing Ukraine's Donbas

events.title: Alan Greenspan, the legendary former Federal Reserve chair, dies
events.description: During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.

events.title: Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack
events.description: Ukraine continues to hit infrastructure on the Crimea to isolate the Russian-annexed peninsula. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown with a ballistic missile in a deadly strike.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack

events.title: Paris suffocates as deadly heatwave takes hold of the country
events.description: France experienced its hottest night on June 22 as Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu said 40 people -- many of them young -- had drowned since June 18 amid a severe heatwave. The deadly heatwave has battered France since last week, disrupting daily life and forcing school closures and train cancellations, with Lecornu convening ministers for a new crisis meeting on June 23 to address the situation. FRANCE 24's Caroline Baum tells us more from Paris.

events.title: Rubio arrives in UAE with aim to head off Gulf Arab unease over tentative Iran deal
events.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is in the United Arab Emirates on the first leg of a three-nation tour of Gulf countries aimed at easing their concerns about the result of an agreement intended to end the war with Iran
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Rubio arrives in UAE with aim to head off Gulf Arab unease over tentative Iran deal

events.title: Missile strike kills three in Ukraine as Russia feels war’s economic strain
events.description: Kyiv has previously accused Moscow of using cluster munitions, which, when dropped, scatter into smaller munitions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Missile strike kills three in Ukraine as Russia feels war’s economic strain

events.title: UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs to be told
events.description: Foreign Office failed to act on warnings of genocide due to ‘pressure’ from emirates, Yale human rights investigator will tell a parliamentary select committee The British government had received intelligence that Ethiopia appeared to be supporting a genocidal militia in Sudan’s civil war as far back as 2024 but did not go public with the news for fear of upsetting the United Arab Emirates (UAE), a parliamentary committee will hear. In May 2024, officials from the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) told Nathaniel Raymond , an American human rights investigator at Yale University, that “significant private pressure” from the UAE meant the UK would not publicly divulge information linking Ethiopia and the emirates to their support for the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs to be told

events.title: Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring
events.description: Major operation launched after spate of pet thefts in Ho Chi Minh City, according to local media Police in Vietnam have rescued more than 400 cats in a bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports. More than 40 cats were reunited with their owners after the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions in which they were found, the groups said. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring

events.title: 2 students in custody after shooting at high school in Philippines kills 3
events.description: An investigation is underway to determine the cause. Police said the suspects claimed they were bullied at school.
events.ticker_label: 💥 2 students in custody after shooting at high school in Philippines kills 3

events.title: Afghan Taliban clamp down on women, smartphones, protests
events.description: The Taliban are tightening the screws on women's clothing, shooting at protesters and ordering even their own officials to stop using smartphones. Behind the regime's omnipresent control, there are signs of deep anxiety.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Afghan Taliban clamp down on women, smartphones, protests

events.title: Kenyan authorities to charge students with murder in dorm fire that killed 16 girls
events.description: Prosecutors in Kenya have prepared murder charges against students accused of starting a deadly dormitory fire in May
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Kenyan authorities to charge students with murder in dorm fire that killed 16 girls

events.title: Armed group in Nigeria kills at least 20 people and exchanges gunfire with police
events.description: Police say an armed group has killed at least 20 people in Nigeria

events.title: Prominent businessman learns fate in boat crash case that killed teen, maimed another
events.description: George Pino was found not guilty in a 2022 Biscayne Bay boat crash that killed one teenager and left another with permanent disabilities.

events.title: Tesla in autopilot mode left roadway and crashed into home, killing 76-year-old woman inside, officials say
events.description: An elderly woman was killed after a Tesla in autopilot mode crashed into a Texas home, according to Harris County officials. Driver was not intoxicated.

events.title: Major dispute to threaten Trump's Iran deal over billions in frozen Tehran funds: expert
events.description: U.S.-Iran talks in Switzerland face their first major test over who controls billions in potentially unfrozen Iranian assets, an analyst warned.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Major dispute to threaten Trump's Iran deal over billions in frozen Tehran funds: expert

events.title: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back
events.description: Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland.

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: Lebanon's lose-lose: Is there another way out of conflict?
events.description: The new US-Iran peace deal seems to be forcing Lebanon to choose between Israel's sphere of influence or Iran's. It wants neither. But could there be a better alternative, achieved with the help of regional diplomacy?

events.title: Wanted: Jobs, critical minerals as Zambia's election nears
events.description: Zambians head to the polls in August with jobs and living costs at the top of their concerns. The outcome could also impact the ongoing global competition for critical minerals and redefine ties with China and the US.

events.title: Schweinsteiger's Ivory Coast comments spark racism debate
events.description: Bastian Schweinsteiger's TV analysis of Germany's World Cup opponents Ivory Coast appeared to draw on racist stereotypes. For some observers, the comments are evidence that racist tropes are still common in football.

events.title: World Cup drives Brazil's online betting boom
events.description: With the World Cup underway, Brazil is experiencing an online betting boom, as over a third of adults are expected to bet on the tournament. The boom comes with consequences, as official figures show a link with mental health issues. A bill is even going through the Brazilian parliament to ban celebrities from advertising for betting companies. FRANCE 24's Jan Onoszko tells us more from Rio de Janeiro.

events.title: Whistleblower investigating Ecuadorian president’s family business was murdered, activists say
events.description: Monika Silva Koniuszek died from a blow to the head and strangulation, a postmortem found, despite government claim of suicide Campaigners in Ecuador say a Polish anti-corruption activist who investigated allegations against the family business of the country’s rightwing president was murdered to silence her. Monika Silva Koniuszek, 41, was found dead in her home in Montañita, a coastal town in Ecuador’s Santa Elena province. The single mother of daughters aged four and nine, was found on the floor with a noose around her neck on 8 June. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Whistleblower investigating Ecuadorian president’s family business was murdered, activists say

events.title: Experts fired by Trump resurrect mothballed climate website
events.description: Fired US federal workers have revived a defunct climate website — pushing back as the Trump administration escalates cuts to publicly funded science and research.

events.title: France's elderly particularly at risk as heatwave suffocates country
events.description: Much of France remains under severe heat alert on June 23 after the country experienced its hottest afternoon and night since records began in 1947. Temperatures around 40 degrees Celsius were forecast for cities including Bordeaux, Rennes and Toulouse. The extreme heat has already proven deadly - two children were found dead inside a parked car on June 22, while 40 people have drowned over the past few days as they sought to cool off.

events.title: Guinness crowns Canberra town crier as the world's loudest person at 122.4 decibels
events.description: An Australian professional air conditioner cleaner and honorary town crier has been recognized as the world’s loudest person

events.title: Connecticut dad jumped to his death during concert at Madison Square Garden in NYC: report
events.description: A Connecticut man allegedly appeared intoxicated before jumping from a balcony at a Goose concert at Madison Square Garden, according to witnesses.

events.title: Los Angeles schools superintendent Alberto Carvalho resigns amid FBI probe
events.description: Alberto Carvalho is stepping down as LAUSD superintendent, citing a desire to refocus on students months after FBI searches and amid a federal investigation.

events.title: Nancy Guthrie ransom note claimed that she had died, been ‘buried with nature,’ source says
events.description: Harvey Levin says reports that Nancy Guthrie's suspected kidnappers apologized for her purported death were not in the ransom note TMZ received.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Nancy Guthrie ransom note claimed that she had died, been ‘buried with nature,’ source says

events.title: LIVE: England vs Ghana – World Cup 2026
events.description: All our updates as Kane's England face Ghana in Group L, with buildup, team news coverage and text commentary stream.

events.title: Mexico exhibition relives memories of Qatar World Cup 2022
events.description: An exhibition in Mexico held during the World Cup explores the legacy of Qatar 2022.

events.title: LIVE: Portugal vs Uzbekistan – World Cup 2026
events.description: Follow live updates as Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal face Uzbekistan, including the build-up and live text commentary.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility
events.description: Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya’s health minister told a court he had ordered preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility to stop, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous order to end work. Many Kenyans strongly oppose the facility , with deadly protests erupting since the complex was announced in May for US citizens evacuated from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is grappling with a widespread Ebola outbreak . Continue reading...

events.title: Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive
events.description: Written in 1947, Kiyoshi Tanimoto’s account of the horrors of the atomic bomb attack will be published in August and is being made into a film The memoir of a man who survived the horrors of Hiroshima is to be published for the first time this summer after its discovery in a US archive. The 230-page memoir was written almost 80 years ago by Kiyoshi Tanimoto, who witnessed the city’s destruction after the atomic bomb was dropped in 1945. He will now be portrayed in a feature film by Takehiro Hira, whose acclaimed roles include the detective in the Netflix Japanese-British drama Giri/Haji. Pre-production begins in November, ahead of the shoot in February 2027. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive

events.title: Cult leader William Kamm and wife groomed young girl for a decade, Sydney court told
events.description: The 76-year-old allegedly ordered he should have 12 wives to ‘repopulate’ the world. He and his wife deny the charges Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The leader of a cult and his wife allegedly groomed a young girl over a decade so she could bear 45 of his children in a post-apocalyptic world. William Kamm, also known as “Little Pebble”, was the leader of the Order of Saint Charbel, a religious cult run from a property on the New South Wales south coast. Continue reading...

events.title: Curacao's trailblazing doctor at the World Cup

events.title: Billionaire to invest in ocean protection as UK and US scale back funding

events.title: Former Pinochet agents convicted over 1976 Washington DC carbomb murder
events.description: Attack targeted former Chile ambassador Orlando Letelier and his US colleague Ronni Karpen Moffitt Fifty years after Gen Augusto Pinochet ’s secret police detonated a car bomb in the heart of Washington DC, killing Orlando Letelier, a former Chilean minister and ambassador to the US, and his American colleague Ronni Karpen Moffitt, a Santiago court has convicted three former agents of Moffitt’s murder. Judge Paola Plaza, a special minister for human rights in Chile, sentenced Pedro Espinoza, José Zara, and Raúl Iturriaga to 15 years in prison for their roles in the killing of Moffitt, 25. Continue reading...

events.title: Montreal mayor calls for end to random police checks amid racial profiling investigation
events.description: City’s police force faces investigation of 16 officers accused of disproportionately targeting Black and Arab residents Montreal’s mayor has called for a halt to random police checks as the city’s police force grapples with an internal investigation into racism and racial profiling by 16 officers. Mayor Soraya Martinez Ferrada told reporters last week that her husband, who is Black, had been repeatedly stopped by police while driving. Continue reading...

events.title: Dutch PM apologises for Moluccan soldiers’ mistreatment after Indonesian independence
events.description: Rob Jetten acknowledges grief and pain of Moluccan families as crowdfunded monument unveiled in Rotterdam The Dutch prime minister, Rob Jetten, has formally apologised for the “heartless” mistreatment of thousands of Moluccan soldiers who fought for the Dutch colonial army during Indonesia’s struggle for independence. About 12,500 people – men who had served in the Royal Dutch East Indies and their families – came from a group of Indonesian islands to the Netherlands in 1951, many having been given no choice. They thought it would be a temporary evacuation after Indonesia had won independence. Continue reading...

events.title: US, Iran give conflicting claims over Hormuz fees
events.description: US top diplomat Rubio said Washington will not accept tolls on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. But Iran has said they want to charge "maritime service fees." And Israel and Lebanon are set for talks in the US.

events.title: Hong Kong pro-democracy publisher Jimmy Lai honored by DW

events.title: Afghanistan in crisis: Drought, malnutrition and a worsening humanitarian situation
events.description: Imagine being one of a family of nine and sitting down to a meal of potato peelings and other scraps, boiled up into a soup. This is the harsh reality for many of Afghanistan’s most vulnerable families, forced on them by climate change and drought, widespread malnutrition and increasing restrictions on women, since the Taliban overran Kabul in 2021.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: U.S. lifts Iran oil sanctions. And, federal judge rules SAVE voter tool unlawful
events.description: The U.S. has temporarily lifted oil sanctions on Iran as peace talks continue. And, a federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's data system, known as SAVE, is unlawful.

events.title: Who would pay for Trump's proposed $300 billion Iran reconstruction fund?
events.description: It's still unclear who would fund a proposed $300 billion reconstruction plan for Iran. Former National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan called the whole approach "something entirely new."

events.title: Scotland: Sturgeon's ex-husband Peter Murrell jailed for 5 years and 3 months

events.title: Turkey arrests more than 200 in crackdown before NATO summit

events.title: Extreme heat forces early closure of Paris tourist attractions
events.description: A severe heatwave gripping France on Tuesday forced the early closure of the Eiffel Tower and the Louvre Museum, two of Paris's most visited tourist attractions, as authorities grappled with soaring temperatures that have disrupted daily life across the country.

events.title: Heatwave: France records hottest-ever night
events.description: France saw its hottest night since records began in 1947 from Monday to Tuesday, with temperatures across the country averaging 21.6C after darkness fell, according to the country’s weather agency. In Paris, some without air conditioning at home spent the night in parks to get relief from the heat.

events.title: French Resistant fighter Marc Bloch honoured in politically charged ceremony
events.description: Historian and Resistance fighter Marc Bloch, who was tortured and executed by the Gestapo in 1944, will enter the Pantheon on Tuesday in a rare tribute reserved for France's most revered historical figures. The ceremony carries political significance less than a year before France heads to the polls to elect a successor to Macron, with Marine Le Pen's far-right National Rally (RN) party eyeing its best chance at seizing power. FRANCE 24's Marc Perelman tells us more on how significant this honour is.

events.title: Alan Greenspan, longtime head of the US Federal Reserve, dies aged 100
events.description: Alan Greenspan, the longtime head of the Federal Reserve and influential economist, has died aged 100.

events.title: ‘Cruel hoax’: Canadian healthcare staff decry employer’s email promising fake day off
events.description: Unions condemn ‘insensitive’ internal cybersecurity test sent to healthcare workers in Newfoundland and Labrador For years, healthcare staff in the Canadian province of Newfoundland and Labrador have felt overworked and underappreciated. Turnover, burnout and thinning resources were pushing workers in the sector to a breaking point. So when the email titled “June Holiday” arrived in thousands of inboxes, they felt a moment of overdue joy. Continue reading...

To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a section of the wall near Beijing. events.title: Labor’s tax deal with the Greens is a crucial win – but its NDIS changes could pay the price
events.description: The victory may have dinged Labor’s chances of easily passing its significant cost-cutting reforms to the NDIS through the Senate in August Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The government scored a crucial win on Tuesday, securing support for its tax changes through the Senate . But that victory may have dinged Labor’s chances of easily passing its other major budget measure – significant cost-cutting reforms to the NDIS – through the Senate in August. Labor’s deal with the Greens sees an inquiry into the NDIS extended for two months, with plans for new public hearings and more time for a committee process, after even the health minister, Mark Butler, admitted he had heard “confronting” evidence from the disability community fearful of losing access to key supports. Eight additional weeks of a public inquiry, spearheaded by an activist party hellbent on trying to kill this bill, is not nothing. Continue reading...

events.title: Angus Taylor won’t say if he supports multiculturalism
events.description: Treasurer says Coalition playing ‘footsie with the monoculture language’ after Liberal leader sidesteps questions at press conference Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Angus Taylor has repeatedly refused to say whether he supports multiculturalism in Australia, sidestepping five questions at a press conference about his views and claiming “there’s all these vague words running around”. The treasurer, Jim Chalmers, accused the opposition leader of “playing footsie” with One Nation and Pauline Hanson’s call for a “monoculture” in Australia , as Labor ministers committed to backing multiculturalism. Continue reading...

events.title: Britain left the EU 10 years ago. Its politics has been an unruly mess

events.title: Europe is spending billions to rearm, so why the delays?
events.description: European countries have been trying to scale up their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

events.title: EU hosts Taliban for migrant return talks in Brussels
events.description: The EU is hosting Taliban officials after Belgium granted temporary visas for talks on returning failed Afghan asylum-seekers. The meeting is one of the bloc's highest-level contacts with the Taliban since 2021.

events.title: Germany news: Merz hails pension reform proposals
events.description: Chancellor Merz says a proposed pension reform package should be fully implemented. A survey has shown that almost half of teachers in Germany find pupils' behavior stressful. DW has more.

events.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
events.description: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

events.title: Brexit: Discord in the UK music industry, 10 years on
events.description: Ten years after the referendum that shook the United Kingdom, what impact has Brexit had on one of its greatest exports: music? From major festivals in the UK to the grassroots venues in Europe determined to host British bands, FRANCE 24's Olivia Salazar-Winspear sets out to meet the people keeping the music scene alive.

events.title: Brexit: The divorce that keeps costing
events.description: As the United Kingdom marks 10 years since the Brexit vote, we take a look at how it has impacted daily life for Brits and – to a degree – Europeans too. From food costs, to postage, to pets and passports, we dive into how Brexit has squeezed pennies and pounds from wallets and added many an administrative headache on both sides of the Channel.

events.title: Spanish PM faces mounting pressure as party mired in political scandals
events.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez said on June 23 that his government would continue its work, despite mounting political pressure following the corruption conviction of his former transport minister a day earlier. His ⁠ruling Socialist Workers' Party ​faces several court cases involving allegations of graft. Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez has not been named in ​any of the cases to date, but more than a dozen people, including his wife and brother and an influential former Socialist premier have been implicated.

events.title: Former Wimbledon champion given four-year suspension

events.description: A European Union mission has highlighted the transparency and efficiency of Colombia's presidential election vote count

events.title: China’s newest aircraft carrier sails through the Taiwan Strait
events.description: China's newest and most powerful aircraft carrier, the Fujian, has sailed through the Taiwan Strait, according to Taiwan's Defense Ministry

events.title: Thai woman faces Myanmar court in an immigration trial tied to US diplomat’s killing
events.description: A Thai woman accused of killing her U.S. diplomat ex-husband has appeared in a Myanmar court
events.ticker_label: 💥 Thai woman faces Myanmar court in an immigration trial tied to US diplomat’s killing

events.title: A legal battle over a former Zambian president's burial might be over
events.description: A South African court has ruled in favor of former Zambian President Edgar Lungu's family regarding his burial

events.title: Lithuanian government steps down after coalition reshuffle
events.description: Lithuanian Prime Minister Inga Ruginienė and her cabinet have stepped down after changes to the ruling coalition

events.title: Man arrested over collecting human body parts taken from cemeteries, police say
events.description: Police in Hungary have arrested a 30-year-old man for collecting human body parts

events.title: Estranged husband of former Scottish leader sentenced to more than 5 years in prison
events.description: The estranged husband of former Scottish First Minister Nicola Sturgeon has been sentenced to five years and three months in prison

events.title: Afghan Taliban to hold rare, closed-door talks with EU officials on deportations
events.description: A Taliban delegation is in Brussels for talks with the European Union, focusing on deportations, according to a Taliban official

events.title: How Brexit broke British politics
events.description: Brexit fractured the European Union, and it broke British politics

events.title: For one young refugee in Uganda, basketball is more than a game
events.description: When 19-year-old Stephane Kulimushi looks around the basketball court in Kampala where he trains young refugees, he sees more than players.

events.title: Austin Metcalf's father haunted by Karmelo Anthony trial visuals released: ‘I had never seen those pictures’
events.description: Convicted killer Karmelo Anthony is fighting to appeal his 35-year sentence for murdering Austin Metcalf at a Texas high school track meet in 2025.

events.title: From the Moon to Mars: Engineering the Future of Human Exploration
events.description: The journey to Mars begins with the Moon. This video explores how Lockheed Martin is helping NASA prepare for the next era of human exploration through Orion, Artemis, robotic missions, lunar resources and technologies designed to help astronauts live, work and travel safely in deep space

events.title: Charlie Kirk assassination suspect's lawyers mirror notorious killer's tactical delay strategy: fmr prosecutor
events.description: Tyler Robinson's defense in the Charlie Kirk assassination case allegedly mirrors Bryan Kohberger's legal playbook, a former prosecutor says.

events.title: FBI brings back fugitive accused in $3.7B Medicare fraud scheme after capture in Turkey
events.description: Fugitive Ibrahim Khaldoon Hilmi captured in Turkey, after allegedly masterminding a $3.7 billion Medicare fraud scheme, one of the largest in U.S. history.

events.title: Turkey detains over 200 suspects, including alleged ISIS militants, in sweeping raid ahead of NATO summit
events.description: Turkey detained more than 200 suspects, including alleged ISIS militants, in a sweeping Ankara raid ahead of the July NATO summit Trump is expected to attend.

events.title: Trump gets major win against China in African rare earth minerals race
events.description: Trump administration backs American mining company Virtus Minerals in the DRC to counter China's control of critical cobalt and copper supply chains.

events.title: Trump says Colombia's 'El Tigre' will be a 'great president' as socialist opponent launches legal challenge
events.description: Trump congratulated de la Espriella on his apparent Colombia election win. With 99.9% counted, he leads 49.7% to 48.7%, but rival Cepeda has launched a challenge over voting irregularities.

events.title: Keith Kellogg tells Iranian dissidents the 'window is open' to force regime change in Tehran
events.description: Lt. Gen. Keith Kellogg urged Iranian dissidents at a Paris NCRI gathering to seize a historic opening, saying the Tehran regime is weaker than ever.

events.title: Meloni's spat with Trump is calculated strategy to boost her approval ratings: expert
events.description: Analyst says Meloni's public feud with President Trump is a calculated strategy to boost her declining approval ratings ahead of Italy's 2027 general election.

events.title: Iran's unprecedented 'whole-regime' delegation at US deal talks signals one goal: expert
events.description: Iran sent a whole-regime delegation, including its central bank governor and oil officials to Switzerland talks, signaling demands for immediate cash flow.

events.title: Trump-backed 'El Tigre' looks to crush cartels, end Colombia's socialist era in pivotal election
events.description: Abelardo de la Espriella, known as El Tigre, rides voter frustration over crime and cartels in Colombia's high-stakes presidential runoff election.

events.title: African and Caribbean leaders call for payments, debt cancellation, formal apologies over slavery
events.description: African and Caribbean leaders adopted a 19-point reparations plan in Ghana demanding financial compensation, debt cancellation and formal apologies.

events.title: H5N1 bird flu confirmed in Australia for the first time, meaning virus has now reached every continent
events.description: Australia confirms its first case of H5N1 bird flu in a seabird near Esperance, Western Australia, meaning the virus has now reached every continent.
